author: gb9t
content:
**Phase B advance: lib/ tier complete.**

.design/lib/00-overview.md (223 lines, 10 REQ / 11 AC, 0 open).

Tier-3 docs spawned (12 planned, none written):
data-structures, strings-fmt, compression, checksums-hashes, lightweight-crypto, math, usercopy, debug-tooling, kunit, vdso-core, specialized-helpers (raid/raid6/reed-solomon/dim/pldmfw/fonts).

Compat-contract slice owned by lib:
- vsprintf %p extension format specifiers (visible in dmesg, ftrace, /proc — exhaustive list documented)
- /sys/kernel/debug/dynamic_debug/control query/set ABI
- KUnit TAP output format (downstream test runners parse it)
- vdso_data struct layout (read by vDSO from userspace)
- Bit-identical compressed output for codec/algorithm/level (so existing on-disk data decompresses correctly)
- Bit-identical CRC and lightweight-hash output

Verification expectations:
- Layer 1: 5 anticipated unsafe-block clusters (maple/xarray walking, iov_iter advance, vsprintf buffer, usercopy raw calls, bitmap raw bit ops)
- Layer 2: 5 mandatory TLA+ models (xarray, maple_tree, stackdepot, vdso_seqlock, dynamic_debug)
- Layer 3: 9 mandatory invariant harnesses (rbtree red-black invariants, list link integrity, maple_tree range invariants, xarray radix invariants, kfifo ring invariants, min_heap heap property, bitmap boundaries, stackdepot chain integrity, iov_iter cursor invariants)
- Layer 4 opt-ins recommended for v0: lightweight-crypto.md (chacha20/poly1305/blake2s/libsha256 via Creusot — textbook algorithms, tractable proofs); checksums-hashes.md (CRC + siphash + xxhash via Creusot)

Most lib/ surface already has rust-for-linux abstractions (list, rbtree, str, iov, io, xarray, kunit) — Rookery extends, doesn't fork. Goal: reduce unsafe count below current rust-for-linux first-cut wrappers where possible.

Coverage status: 2/14 in-scope subsystems have Tier-2 overviews. Continuing to mm/00-overview.md (the next major).

All upstream paths verified to resolve under /home/doll/linux-src.
